title: Data coverage, six expense lines, and bank tie-up — checklist
disclaimer: This is a working checklist for collection and reconciliation. It does not replace audit. Bank ↔ ledger matching is not auto-run in this app until you supply tagged exports.
understood_rules: Rent: company rent is paid only directly to Harshad Mehta — never treat payments made only to Nakul Mehta as rent.
Nakul Mehta: payments to Nakul are reimbursements — each must reconcile to expenses Nakul incurred (ledgers, tagged bank/CC, vouchers), not to the rent line.
Space Within P&L workbook is FY 2024-25 framing; your reconciliation start date is 15-Sep-2024 (workbook main sheet is Oct'24–Mar'25 — align or restate opening balances for 15-Sep–30-Sep).
SW books carry expenses only through 30-Jun-2025; after that expenses are on ECPL, not SW.
ECPL P&L (1-Apr-2025 to 28-Feb-2026) remains the main authority for ECPL operating expenses in that window.
You flagged ~three months where complete expense detail (vouchers / line backup) is still missing — exact months to confirm with finance (see below).
Target expense categories for bank reconciliation: Rent, MSEDCL (Torrent electricity), Salary (ECPL from Apr'25), Food Exp, Water Bill, Petty cash.

period_notes.sw_pl_workbook_sheet_period: P&L_Oct 24 to Mar 25_Space — 6 months Oct'24–Mar'25 in file title
period_notes.reconciliation_anchor: 15-Sep-2024 per stakeholder — gap vs workbook: need opening Sep'24 (partial month) treatment

internal_inconsistency_flag.topic: Space Within — Rent (same workbook, two sheets)
internal_inconsistency_flag.revised_note: Revised PL_Oct to Mar 25 — Rent Oct To March ₹6,00,000
internal_inconsistency_flag.action: Resolve which rent total is correct for Oct'24–Mar'25 before bank tie-up.

data_we_have: ECPL monthly rent row (11 months in P&L workbook export)
SW P&L + Revised summary (conflicting rent — see flag)

Exclude Nakul Mehta beneficiary lines from rent roll-up; map those to reimbursement schedule
Written lease map: which entity (SW vs ECPL) paid which months to Harshad

data_we_have: ECPL MSEDCL + MSEDCL F1 rows (FY 25–26 P&L monthly)
data_missing: Torrent/MSEDCL bills vs each bank debit (SW HDFC + ECPL Axis)
Clarify F1 split vs main MSEDCL in books

data_we_have: ECPL monthly salary Apr'25–Feb'26 (per your note: salary from Apr'25 on ECPL sheet)
Multiple salary detail sheets inside SW Excel (not exported to app)
data_missing: Bank salary batches vs payroll for SW (through Jun'25) and ECPL
PF/ESI/challan tie if required for tax
Apr–Jun 2025 overlap: confirm last SW salary month vs first ECPL salary month

six_expense_lines.space_within_pl_oct_mar: Not a separate line on SW formal P&L (likely inside Petty / not split)
data_missing: SW-side food if booked only through petty — need petty categorisation or separate ledger
Municipal/water vendor bills vs bank debits

data_we_have: ECPL petty total in monthly export
Detailed petty sheet in SW Excel (1096+ rows — not in app JSON)
data_missing: Month-by-month petty rollforward tied to cash withdrawals + imprest
Every petty line matched to bank outflow or physical cash voucher

three_months_missing_detail.user_note: You reported ~3 months of complete expense detail still missing (while SW expenses exist only until Jun-2025).
please_confirm_exact_months: Option A: Jul, Aug, Sep 2025 (post SW cessation — ECPL-only, if voucher pack incomplete)
Option B: Another fiscal quarter — specify
Option C: Sep 2024 partial + two other months — if 15-Sep anchor drives restatement
three_months_missing_detail.action: Reply with exact month triple; we will label snapshots accordingly.

what_we_have_in_app_now: ECPL P&L + monthly + sales/purchase summaries + printed-invoice cross-check (JSON under snapshots/ecpl_fy2526/)
Space Within formal P&L Oct–Mar + Revised summary (JSON under snapshots/sw_fy2425/)
ECPL Axis slices: Harshad/Nakul payments, STPL credits (JSON under snapshots/bank/)

nakul_reimbursement_track.rule: All payments to Nakul Mehta (not Harshad) = reimbursement; match to expense evidence.
data_we_have: ECPL P&L indirect lines paid by Nakul (Odoo, internet, etc.) in workbook export
Nakul HDFC CC / savings tagged CSVs (under Downloads/ORIGINALS — not all copied into app)
data_missing: Single reimbursement_ledger: date, bank_ref, amount_to_nakul, linked_expense_voucher_ids
ECPL Axis + SW HDFC exports with payee tag Nakul vs Harshad vs vendor

what_is_missing_for_full_bank_tie: Space Within HDFC: export JSON snapshot into public/data/snapshots/bank/ with narration tags for Rent, Torrent, Salary, Food, Water, Petty, Tax, STPL, ECPL
ECPL Axis: same six-line tags for Apr'25 onwards
Unmatched bank report: debits/credits with no voucher / ledger hit (generate from bank CSV + Tally export)
Vendor outstanding: aged payables from Tally as at 31-Mar-2026 (or chosen date)
Resolution of SW Rent ₹1L vs ₹6L conflict between sheets

unreconciled_bank_payments.explanation: The app does not yet run row-level bank ↔ ledger matching. Provide: (1) bank CSV with stable narration, (2) payment register or Tally export with voucher numbers, (3) optional match file: bank_ref,amount,date,voucher_no,status.
known_slices_in_app: ECPL Axis — rent (beneficiary Harshad) vs reimbursement (beneficiary Nakul only) — see bank/ecpl_axis_harshad_payments.json

collect: Creditor ageing (SW + ECPL) as at reporting date
List of vendors with GRN but no payment / partial payment
Any card payments (Nakul CC) mapped to ECPL expense lines

harshad_income_tax_proxy.basis_pl_loaded: P&L_Oct 24 to Mar 25_Space — Nett Profit (same period as sheet, not adjusted to 15-Sep yet)
harshad_income_tax_proxy.caveat: Profit must be restated if (a) 15-Sep-2024 opening differs, (b) Revised PL logic replaces formal P&L, or (c) period extended beyond Mar'25 for SW. Cross-check actual advance/self-assessment paid on SW HDFC.

next_files_to_send: Space Within HDFC FY 24-25 + FY 25-26 through Jun-2025 (CSV or JSON snapshot for app)
ECPL Axis FY 25-26 full CSV (already partially parsed — refresh if new months)
Tally payment / petty cash voucher export for the three missing-detail months (once months are named)
